              SERVINGMAN
text: Where’s Potpan that he helps not

              SERVINGMAN
text: to take away? He shift a trencher? He scrape a

speaker:
FIRST

text: trencher?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: When good manners shall lie

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: all in one or two men’s hands, and they unwashed

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: too, ’tis a foul thing.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Away with the joint stools, remove

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: the court cupboard, look to the plate.—

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Good thou, save me a piece of marchpane, and, as

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: thou loves me, let the porter let in Susan Grindstone

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: and Nell.—Anthony and Potpan!

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Ay, boy, ready.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: You are looked for and called for,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: asked for and sought for, in the great chamber.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: We cannot be here and there too.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: Cheerly, boys! Be brisk awhile, and the longer liver

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: take all.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Welcome, gentlemen. Ladies that have their toes

speaker: CAPULET
text: Unplagued with corns will walk a bout with you.—

speaker: CAPULET
text: Ah, my mistresses, which of you all

speaker: CAPULET
text: Will now deny to dance? She that makes dainty,

speaker: CAPULET
text: She, I’ll swear, hath corns. Am I come near you now?—

speaker: CAPULET
text: Welcome, gentlemen. I have seen the day

speaker: CAPULET
text: That I have worn a visor and could tell

speaker: CAPULET
text: A whispering tale in a fair lady’s ear,

speaker: CAPULET
text: Such as would please. ’Tis gone, ’tis gone, ’tis gone.

speaker: CAPULET
text: You are welcome, gentlemen.—Come, musicians, play.

speaker: CAPULET
text: A hall, a hall, give room!—And foot it, girls.—

speaker: CAPULET
text: More light, you knaves, and turn the tables up,

speaker: CAPULET
text: And quench the fire; the room is grown too hot.—

speaker: CAPULET
text: Ah, sirrah, this unlooked-for sport comes well.—

speaker: CAPULET
text: Nay, sit, nay, sit, good cousin Capulet,

speaker: CAPULET
text: For you and I are past our dancing days.

speaker: CAPULET
text: How long is ’t now since last yourself and I

speaker: CAPULET
text: Were in a mask?

speaker:
CAPULET’S
               
              COUSIN
text: By ’r Lady, thirty years.

speaker: CAPULET
text: What, man, ’tis not so much, ’tis not so much.

speaker: CAPULET
text: ’Tis since the nuptial of Lucentio,

speaker: CAPULET
text: Come Pentecost as quickly as it will,

speaker: CAPULET
text: Some five and twenty years, and then we masked.

speaker:
CAPULET’S
               
              COUSIN
text: ’Tis more, ’tis more. His son is elder, sir.

speaker:
CAPULET’S
               
              COUSIN
text: His son is thirty.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Will you tell me that?

speaker: CAPULET
text: His son was but a ward two years ago.

speaker: ROMEO
text: What lady’s that which doth enrich the hand

speaker: ROMEO
text: Of yonder knight?

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: I know not, sir.

speaker: ROMEO
text: O, she doth teach the torches to burn bright!

speaker: ROMEO
text: It seems she hangs upon the cheek of night

speaker: ROMEO
text: As a rich jewel in an Ethiop’s ear—

speaker: ROMEO
text: Beauty too rich for use, for Earth too dear.

speaker: ROMEO
text: So shows a snowy dove trooping with crows

speaker: ROMEO
text: As yonder lady o’er her fellows shows.

speaker: ROMEO
text: The measure done, I’ll watch her place of stand

speaker: ROMEO
text: And, touching hers, make blessèd my rude hand.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Did my heart love till now? Forswear it, sight,

speaker: ROMEO
text: For I ne’er saw true beauty till this night.

speaker: TYBALT
text: This, by his voice, should be a Montague.—

speaker: TYBALT
text: Fetch me my rapier, boy.

speaker: TYBALT
text: What, dares the slave

speaker: TYBALT
text: Come hither covered with an antic face

speaker: TYBALT
text: To fleer and scorn at our solemnity?

speaker: TYBALT
text: Now, by the stock and honor of my kin,

speaker: TYBALT
text: To strike him dead I hold it not a sin.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Why, how now, kinsman? Wherefore storm you so?

speaker: TYBALT
text: Uncle, this is a Montague, our foe,

speaker: TYBALT
text: A villain that is hither come in spite

speaker: TYBALT
text: To scorn at our solemnity this night.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Young Romeo is it?

speaker: TYBALT
text: ’Tis he, that villain Romeo.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Content thee, gentle coz. Let him alone.

speaker: CAPULET
text: He bears him like a portly gentleman,

speaker: CAPULET
text: And, to say truth, Verona brags of him

speaker: CAPULET
text: To be a virtuous and well-governed youth.

speaker: CAPULET
text: I would not for the wealth of all this town

speaker: CAPULET
text: Here in my house do him disparagement.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Therefore be patient. Take no note of him.

speaker: CAPULET
text: It is my will, the which if thou respect,

speaker: CAPULET
text: Show a fair presence and put off these frowns,

speaker: CAPULET
text: An ill-beseeming semblance for a feast.

speaker: TYBALT
text: It fits when such a villain is a guest.

speaker: TYBALT
text: I’ll not endure him.

speaker: CAPULET
text: He shall be endured.

speaker: CAPULET
text: What, goodman boy? I say he shall. Go to.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Am I the master here or you? Go to.

speaker: CAPULET
text: You’ll not endure him! God shall mend my soul,

speaker: CAPULET
text: You’ll make a mutiny among my guests,

speaker: CAPULET
text: You will set cock-a-hoop, you’ll be the man!

speaker: TYBALT
text: Why, uncle, ’tis a shame.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Go to, go to.

speaker: CAPULET
text: You are a saucy boy. Is ’t so indeed?

speaker: CAPULET
text: This trick may chance to scathe you. I know what.

speaker: CAPULET
text: You must contrary me. Marry, ’tis time—

speaker: CAPULET
text: Well said, my hearts.—You are a princox, go.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Be quiet, or—More light, more light!—for shame,

speaker: CAPULET
text: I’ll make you quiet.—What, cheerly, my hearts!

speaker: TYBALT
text: Patience perforce with willful choler meeting

speaker: TYBALT
text: Makes my flesh tremble in their different greeting.

speaker: TYBALT
text: I will withdraw, but this intrusion shall,

speaker: TYBALT
text: Now seeming sweet, convert to bitt’rest gall.

speaker: ROMEO
text: If I profane with my unworthiest hand

speaker: ROMEO
text: This holy shrine, the gentle sin is this:

speaker: ROMEO
text: My lips, two blushing pilgrims, ready stand

speaker: ROMEO
text: To smooth that rough touch with a tender kiss.

speaker: JULIET
text: Good pilgrim, you do wrong your hand too much,

speaker: JULIET
text: Which mannerly devotion shows in this;

speaker: JULIET
text: For saints have hands that pilgrims’ hands do touch,

speaker: JULIET
text: And palm to palm is holy palmers’ kiss.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Have not saints lips, and holy palmers too?

speaker: JULIET
text: Ay, pilgrim, lips that they must use in prayer.

speaker: ROMEO
text: O then, dear saint, let lips do what hands do.

speaker: ROMEO
text: They pray: grant thou, lest faith turn to despair.

speaker: JULIET
text: Saints do not move, though grant for prayers’ sake.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Then move not while my prayer’s effect I take.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Thus from my lips, by thine, my sin is purged.

speaker: JULIET
text: Then have my lips the sin that they have took.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Sin from my lips? O trespass sweetly urged!

speaker: ROMEO
text: Give me my sin again.

speaker: JULIET
text: You kiss by th’ book.

speaker: NURSE
text: Madam, your mother craves a word with you.

speaker: ROMEO
text: What is her mother?

speaker: NURSE
text: Marry, bachelor,

speaker: NURSE
text: Her mother is the lady of the house,

speaker: NURSE
text: And a good lady, and a wise and virtuous.

speaker: NURSE
text: I nursed her daughter that you talked withal.

speaker: NURSE
text: I tell you, he that can lay hold of her

speaker: NURSE
text: Shall have the chinks.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Is she a Capulet?

speaker: ROMEO
text: O dear account! My life is my foe’s debt.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: Away, begone. The sport is at the best.

speaker: ROMEO
text: Ay, so I fear. The more is my unrest.

speaker: CAPULET
text: Nay, gentlemen, prepare not to be gone.

speaker: CAPULET
text: We have a trifling foolish banquet towards.—

speaker: CAPULET
text: Is it e’en so? Why then, I thank you all.

speaker: CAPULET
text: I thank you, honest gentlemen. Good night.—

speaker: CAPULET
text: More torches here.—Come on then, let’s to bed.—

speaker: CAPULET
text: Ah, sirrah, by my fay, it waxes late.

speaker: CAPULET
text: I’ll to my rest.

speaker: JULIET
text: Come hither, nurse. What is yond gentleman?

speaker: NURSE
text: The son and heir of old Tiberio.

speaker: JULIET
text: What’s he that now is going out of door?

speaker: NURSE
text: Marry, that, I think, be young Petruchio.

speaker: JULIET
text: What’s he that follows here, that would not dance?

speaker: NURSE
text: I know not.

speaker: JULIET
text:
Go ask his name.
                 
                The
                 
                Nurse
                 
                goes
                .
               If he be marrièd,

speaker: JULIET
text: My grave is like to be my wedding bed.

speaker: NURSE
text: His name is Romeo, and a Montague,

speaker: NURSE
text: The only son of your great enemy.

speaker: JULIET
text: My only love sprung from my only hate!

speaker: JULIET
text: Too early seen unknown, and known too late!

speaker: JULIET
text: Prodigious birth of love it is to me

speaker: JULIET
text: That I must love a loathèd enemy.

speaker: NURSE
text: What’s this? What’s this?

speaker: JULIET
text: A rhyme I learned even now

speaker: JULIET
text: Of one I danced withal.

speaker: NURSE
text: Anon, anon.

speaker: NURSE
text: Come, let’s away. The strangers all are gone.
